2606.08131.title: LCAM: A Framework for Diagnosing Interactional Alignment Failures in Con-versational AI
2606.08131.paper_summary: Conversational AI is increasingly used for advice, interpretation, reassurance, and decision support in contexts where users may be vulnerable, uncertain, or dependent on the system's apparent competence. Existing alignment work often focuses on model objectives, preference optimization, or output correctness. Yet, many harms arise through interaction: how systems frame authority, express uncertainty, simulate empathy, support reasoning, and make boundaries legible. This paper introduces the Layered Cognitive Alignment Model (LCAM), a conceptual and normative framework for diagnosing interac-tional alignment failures in conversational AI. LCAM defines alignment as a calibrated fit among system behavior, user goals, task demands, and normative context. It distinguishes five layers of fit: perceptual, semantic, affective, cognitive, and ethical, and two diagnostic polarities of misalignment: underfit and overreach. We apply LCAM to a published LLM counseling example, showing how an apparently supportive response can reinforce harmful beliefs, simulate inappropriate care, and obscure role boundaries. By translating conversational failures into audit and governance questions concerning over-reliance, false intimacy, autonomy erosion, boundary confusion, and inappropriate trust, LCAM offers a theoretical and normative lens for evaluating conversational AI beyond accuracy, helpfulness, or trust.
2606.08131.paper_summary_zh: 對話式人工智慧越來越多地用於提供建議、解釋、安撫和決策支持，特別是在用戶可能處於脆弱、不確定或依賴於系統表現能力的情境中。現有的對齊工作通常集中在模型目標、偏好優化或輸出正確性上。然而，許多危害是通過互動產生的：系統如何框架權威、表達不確定性、模擬同理心、支持推理以及使邊界清晰可見。本文介紹了分層認知對齊模型（Layered Cognitive Alignment Model, LCAM），這是一個用於診斷對話式人工智慧中互動對齊失敗的概念性和規範性框架。LCAM將對齊定義為系統行為、用戶目標、任務需求和規範背景之間的校準適配。它區分了五個適配層次：感知、語意、情感、認知和倫理，以及兩個不對齊的診斷極性：不足適配和過度延伸。我們將LCAM應用於一個已發表的LLM諮詢案例，展示了看似支持的回應如何強化有害信念、模擬不當的關懷並模糊角色邊界。通過將對話失敗轉化為關於過度依賴、虛假親密、自主性侵蝕、邊界混淆和不當信任的審計和治理問題，LCAM提供了一個理論和規範的視角，以評估對話式人工智慧超越準確性、有用性或信任。
2606.08131.author: Manuele Reani et.al.
